prompt:
User request:
Generate a scatter plot of sales vs customer_age colored by region with a Streamlit selectbox for product

Available columns:
product: category
revenue: float
region: category
sales: float
customer_age: int
output:
import streamlit as st
import plotly.express as px

st.title("Sales vs Customer Age")

options = ["All"] + sorted(df["product"].dropna().unique().tolist())
selected_product = st.selectbox("Product", options)
if selected_product == "All":
    df_widget = df
else:
    df_widget = df[df["product"] == selected_product]

chart = px.scatter(
    df_widget,
    x="customer_age",
    y="sales",
    color="region",
    template="plotly_white"
)

st.plotly_chart(chart, use_container_width=True)

prompt:
User request:
Visualize a heatmap of count by segment and product where segment == Consumer

Columns in df:
product: category
segment: category
date: datetime
customer_age: int
units: int
output:
import streamlit as st
import plotly.express as px

st.title("Count of Segment vs Product")

df_filtered = df[df["segment"] == "Consumer"]

df_plot = df_filtered.groupby(["segment", "product"], as_index=False).size()

fig = px.imshow(
    df_plot.pivot(index="product", columns="segment", values="size"),
    labels=dict(x="Segment", y="Product", color="Count"),
    x=df_plot["segment"].unique(),
    y=df_plot["product"].unique(),
    color_continuous_scale="Blues",
    template="simple_white"
)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=False)

    y=df_plot["product"].unique(),

    template="plotly_white"
)

Show a box plot of units by region where segment == Consumer with a Streamlit selectbox for segment

Columns in df:
year: int
sales: float
region: category
units: int
segment: category
output:
import streamlit as st
import plotly.express as px

st.header("Units by Region")

options = ["All"] + sorted(df["segment"].dropna().unique().tolist())
selected_segment = st.selectbox("Segment", options)

if selected_segment == "All":
    df_filtered = df
else:
    df_filtered = df[df["segment"] == selected_segment]

chart = px.box(
    df_filtered,
    x="region",
    y="units",
    template="plotly"
)

st.plotly_chart(chart, use_container_width=True)

prompt:
User request:
Plot a heatmap of count by segment and region

Available columns:
revenue: float
date: datetime
segment: category
region: category
year: int
output:
import streamlit as st
import plotly.express as px

st.subheader("Count of Segment vs Region")

df_plot = df.groupby(["segment", "region"], as_index=False).size()

pivot_df = df_plot.pivot(index="region", columns="segment", values="size").fillna(0)

chart = px.imshow(
    pivot_df,
    labels=dict(x="Segment", y="Region", color="Count"),
    x=pivot_df.columns,
    y=pivot_df.index,
    color_continuous_scale="Blues",
    template="plotly_white"
)

st.plotly_chart(chart, use_container_width=False)

User request:
Visualize a line chart of sum customer_age over month

Dataframe columns:
region: category
customer_age: int
year: int
month: category
profit: float
output:
import streamlit as st
import plotly.express as px

st.subheader("Sum Customer Age over Month")

df_plot = df.groupby("month", as_index=False)["customer_age"].sum()

month_order = ["January", "February", "March", "April", "May", "June", 
               "July", "August", "September", "October", "November", "December"]
if df_plot["month"].dtype.name == "category":
    df_plot["month"] = df_plot["month"].astype(str)

existing_months = df_plot["month"].unique()
ordered_months = [m for m in month_order if m in existing_months]
if len(ordered_months) == len(existing_months):
    df_plot["month"] = pd.Categorical(df_plot["month"], categories=ordered_months, ordered=True)
    df_plot = df_plot.sort_values("month")

plot_fig = px.line(
    df_plot,
    x="month",
    y="customer_age",
    template="plotly_white"
)

plot_fig.update_layout(height=500)

st.plotly_chart(plot_fig, use_container_width=True)

)

prompt:

Create a line chart of sum profit over date colored by product where product == C

product: category
date: datetime
month: category
year: int
output:
import streamlit as st
import plotly.express as px

st.title("Sum Profit over Date")

df_filtered = df[df["product"] == "C"]

df_plot = df_filtered.groupby("date", as_index=False).agg({"profit": "sum", "product": "first"})

fig = px.line(

    x="date",
    y="profit",
    color="product"
)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

prompt:
User request:
Build a heatmap of count by region and segment where segment == Consumer with a Streamlit selectbox for region

Columns in df:
revenue: float
region: category
segment: category
customer_age: int
units: int
output:
import streamlit as st
import plotly.express as px

st.header("Count of Region vs Segment")

df_filtered = df[df["segment"] == "Consumer"]

options = ["All"] + sorted(df["region"].dropna().unique().tolist())
selected_region = st.selectbox("Region", options)
if selected_region == "All":
    df_filtered_widget = df_filtered
else:
    df_filtered_widget = df_filtered[df_filtered["region"] == selected_region]

df_plot = df_filtered_widget.groupby(["region", "segment"]).size().reset_index(name="count")

plot_fig = px.imshow(
    df_plot.pivot(index="segment", columns="region", values="count").fillna(0),
    labels=dict(x="Region", y="Segment", color="Count"),
    x=df_plot["region"].unique(),
    y=df_plot["segment"].unique(),
    template="simple_white",
    color_continuous_scale="Blues"
)

st.plotly_chart(plot_fig, use_container_width=False)

segment: category
year: int
revenue: float

output:
import streamlit as st
import plotly.express as px

st.header("Units vs Revenue")

options = ["All"] + sorted(df["segment"].dropna().unique().tolist())
selected_segment = st.selectbox("Segment", options)
if selected_segment == "All":
    df_widget = df
else:
    df_widget = df[df["segment"] == selected_segment]

chart = px.scatter(
    df_widget,
    x="units",
    y="revenue",
    color="segment",
    template="simple_white"
)

chart.update_layout(height=500)

st.plotly_chart(chart, use_container_width=True)

prompt:
User request:
Create a box plot of sales by region where year == 2023 with a Streamlit selectbox for region

Columns in df:
region: category
revenue: float
profit: float
sales: float
year: int
output:
import streamlit as st
import plotly.express as px

st.header("Sales by Region")

df_filtered = df[df["year"] == 2023]

options = ["All"] + sorted(df_filtered["region"].dropna().unique().tolist())
selected_region = st.selectbox("Region", options)

if selected_region == "All":
    df_filtered_widget = df_filtered
else:
    df_filtered_widget = df_filtered[df_filtered["region"] == selected_region]

fig = px.box(
    df_filtered_widget,
    x="region",
    y="sales",
    template="plotly"
)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)
